summary: Verified WhySheet 404 fix end-to-end. Backend: ball_id format is consistent ('<match>-i<inn>-o<over>.<ball>') in latest_ball_id and momentum. /api/ratings/{match}/{player} returns 200 with valid at_ball_id, without at_ball_id, and with nonsense at_ball_id (falls back to latest). Frontend: WhySheet opens with real content on both historical (1535465) and featured (1359487) matches — headlines populated, positives/negatives lists populated, NO 404 error banner.

ui_bugs.component: MomentCard (inside WhySheet 'The Moment that Mattered Most')
ui_bugs.issue: Displays 'Over NaN.4' — the over number renders as NaN. Likely the moment object's over field is missing/null in the API response, so `${over}.${ball}` becomes 'NaN.4'.

issues: Console warnings: recharts container width/height = -1 on initial render (chart briefly has 0 dimensions). Cosmetic; charts render fine after mount.

action_items: Fix 'Over NaN.4' in moment card — ensure Moment.over is populated (or default) in adapter.get_moments / MomentCard renderer
Optional: address recharts container -1 dimension warnings by setting minWidth/minHeight on ResponsiveContainer
critical_code_review_comments: adapter._ball_uid correctly synthesizes canonical id; both momentum and latest_ball_id use it — good.
get_rating_breakdown fallback (unknown at_ball_id -> latest snapshot) is implemented and works (verified with at_ball_id=xxxxx returning 200).
MomentumPoint ball_id also now uses _ball_uid — consistent.

success_rate.backend: 100% (5/5 core tests; 1 test-assertion adjustment needed, not a product bug)
success_rate.frontend: 100% for WhySheet fix; 1 unrelated cosmetic bug (NaN over)

context_for_next_testing_agent: WhySheet 404 fix verified fully. New minor issue: moment.over renders as NaN inside MomentCard shown in WhySheet — investigate Moment schema/adapter mapping if fixing.
rca of the issue: Fixed already by main agent. Root cause: balls collection stored ball_uid as '<m>-i<inn>-<legal_num>' while ratings_snapshots/moments used '<m>-i<inn>-o<over>.<ball>'. Adapter now uniformly synthesizes the latter via _ball_uid() for BallEvent, MomentumPoint, and latest_ball_id; get_rating_breakdown falls back to latest snapshot on unknown ball_id.
